skill.name: game-changing-features
skill.description: Guides strategic product sessions to identify transformative 10x opportunities, quick wins, and prioritized feature bets.
skill.summary: Find high-leverage product opportunities and prioritize the moves most likely to change user value.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static command execution, weak cryptography, network reconnaissance, and system reconnaissance alerts to be false positives caused by markdown formatting, examples, paths, and 10x product-strategy language. No executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, network probing, credential access, or prompt-injection override was found. The skill has a low risk because it instructs agents to save strategy outputs under .claude/docs.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The listed command execution, weak cryptography, network reconnaissance, and system reconnaissance alerts occur in markdown examples, inline paths, headings, and ordinary product-strategy text. No executable Ruby, shell command, cryptographic algorithm, network scan, system scan, or malicious instruction was found in README.md or SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged files contain documentation and skill instructions only. The reviewed lines show markdown fences, paths, examples, and 10x wording rather than executable code or dangerous behavior.
low_findings.recommendation: Treat the static detections as false positives. Keep monitoring future versions for added scripts, tool calls, or network behavior.

low_findings.title: Local Documentation Output Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to write strategy session outputs to .claude/docs/ai/<product-or-area>/10x/session-N.md. This is a local documentation workflow and not exfiltration, but it can create or modify files in the workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The output path is explicitly documented in both files. The behavior is limited to local markdown notes and has no network or secret access path.
low_findings.recommendation: Publish with a note that the skill may create local strategy documents under .claude/docs.

content.value_statement: Teams often default to small feature requests and miss larger product opportunities. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured 10x strategy session with prioritized next moves.
actual_capabilities: Frames the current product value before generating new feature ideas.
Explores massive, medium, and small opportunities across ten product categories.
Scores ideas by impact, reach, frequency, differentiation, defensibility, and feasibility.
Separates quick wins, high-leverage work, strategic bets, and backlog items.
Produces a structured strategy document with assumptions, questions, and next steps.
Encourages evidence-based reasoning from the codebase, product context, or research.
limitations: It does not implement features or write production code.
It does not replace customer interviews, analytics, or market validation.
Its recommendations depend on the product context supplied by the user.
It may create local markdown notes under .claude/docs when used as written.

use_cases.title: Prioritize a Product Roadmap
use_cases.description: Turn scattered feature ideas into ranked quick wins, strategic bets, and backlog candidates.

use_cases.title: Find Startup Growth Bets
use_cases.description: Identify product moves that could create stronger retention, differentiation, or word-of-mouth growth.

use_cases.title: Improve Developer Tools
use_cases.description: Analyze a technical workflow and find automation, collaboration, and visibility improvements with clear tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic 10x Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the game-changing-features skill for this product: [product name]. Current state: [short description]. Find 10x opportunities and rank the best next moves.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Specific Product Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a 10x analysis for [product area]. Focus on current user pain, quick wins, medium opportunities, and one strategic bet worth validating.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Opportunities Against Constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate 10x opportunities for [product]. Constraints: [team size], [timeline], [technical limits]. Prioritize ideas by impact, feasibility, and defensibility.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Evidence-Based Strategy Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the codebase and available product context, then produce a full 10x strategy document for [area]. Include assumptions, unanswered questions, risks, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: What would make our analytics dashboard 10x better?
output_examples.output: A prioritized strategy brief with current value, major opportunities, small high-impact improvements, scores, risks, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.input: Find high-leverage improvements for our API testing tool.
output: Do Now: streamline a repeated workflow.
Do Next: add collaboration around shared test suites.
Explore: investigate AI-assisted test creation after validation.

output_examples.input: We have six weeks and two engineers. What should we build next?
output_examples.output: A constraint-aware ranking that separates fast validation work from larger bets that need more research or staffing.

best_practices: Provide product context, target users, current workflows, and known constraints before starting.
Ask the agent to cite evidence from the codebase, research, or user feedback for each major idea.
Validate one or two top recommendations with users before committing roadmap capacity.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for customer discovery or product analytics.
Do not ask for implementation details before the strategic priority is clear.
Do not accept generic ideas without a specific user problem, expected impact, and validation path.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on strategy, prioritization, and product opportunity discovery.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Founders, product managers, engineering leads, and teams deciding what to build next can use it.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the product area, current state, target users, constraints, and any known customer pain points.

faq.question: Can it inspect my codebase?
faq.answer: Yes, it can guide the agent to research the codebase for product context when the tool supports file access.

faq.question: Will it make final roadmap decisions?
faq.answer: No. It recommends priorities and tradeoffs, but the team must validate and decide.

faq.question: Where are outputs saved?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to save session notes under .claude/docs/ai/<product-or-area>/10x/.
